tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.

body.description: Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

labels.description: Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.

parent.description: Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.

title.description: Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.

tools.name: create_issue

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue
tools.name: dispatch_triage

tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.name: get_commit

tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_file_contents

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_latest_release

annotations.title: Get a release by tag name

tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_release_by_tag

tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_tag

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_branches

tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.name: list_commits

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_releases

tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_tags

tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.name: search_code

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.

sort.description: Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_repositories

Co-authored-by: jorgerangel-msft <102122018+jorgerangel-msft@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"Copilot","email":"198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-04-22T19:55:29Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-22T19:55:29Z"}},"author":{"login":"Copilot","id":198982749,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/copilot-swe-agent","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/1143301?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Remove Azure.Identity from cloudmachine projects (batch 7) (#58463)

* Remove Azure.Identity from cloudmachine projects (batch 7)

Adds per-package override of Azure.AI.Projects to 2.1.0-beta.1, which no longer transitively pulls Azure.Identity. The CPM version (2.0.0 GA) pulls Azure.Identity 1.20.0, conflicting with the type-forwards in Azure.Core 1.53.

Once Azure.AI.Projects 2.1.0 GA ships, the override files should be cleaned up and the central version bumped.

Identity types remain available via Azure.Core 1.53 type-forwards.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: combine Azure.AI.Projects override into existing conditional ItemGroup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove unused Azure.Provisioning.Deployment ref from Provisioning.Tests

The package was referenced but never used in test code (no using statements, no API calls). It was the transitive source of Azure.Identity 1.12.0 that was causing CS0433 in CI after the src project's Azure.Identity ref was removed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add tracking issue link to Azure.AI.Projects override files (#58510)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: JonathanCrd <17486462+JonathanCrd@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"Copilot","email":"198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-04-22T19:18:38Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-22T19:18:38Z"}},"author":{"login":"Copilot","id":198982749,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/copilot-swe-agent","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/1143301?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"[AgentServer] Platform headers, persistence resilience, extensible error codes, logging fix (#58396)

* Fix FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy crash on transport failures

- Fix InvalidOperationException when accessing message.Response after
  transport-level failures (DNS, connection refused, timeout)
- Log full exception (type + message + stack trace) at Error level for
  transport failures using exception filter pattern
- Add traceparent header to all log messages for OTEL distributed trace
  correlation
- Handle edge case of no response + no exception (pipeline short-circuit)
- Add tests for transport failure and traceparent logging

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [AgentServer] Centralize platform headers, add x-request-id, simplify Tier 3 API

- Add PlatformHeaders static class in Core with all cross-protocol header
  name constants (x-request-id, x-platform-server, x-agent-session-id,
  isolation keys, traceparent, x-ms-client-request-id)
- Add RequestIdMiddleware that sets x-request-id response header on every
  HTTP response (OTEL trace ID → incoming header → GUID)
- Add RequestIdBaggagePropagator for distributed tracing correlation
- Enrich Responses error bodies with additionalInfo.request_id matching
  the x-request-id response header
- Add ApiErrorResult for mutable error responses before serialization
- Replace 6 individual Add/Use extension methods with single
  AddAgentServerCore() / UseAgentServerCore() pair
- AgentHostBuilder now dogfoods the public extensions
- Remove IsolationContext header name constants (use PlatformHeaders)
- Remove x-ms-request-id from Foundry storage logging
- Fix FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy crash on transport failures
- Add traceparent to all Foundry storage log messages
- Bump Core to 1.0.0-beta.23

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(Responses): Convert ResponseErrorCode to extensible struct, fix regen cascading errors

- Convert ResponseErrorCode from enum to extensible struct via CodeGenType stub
- Regenerate TypeSpec contracts with type renames (OutputItemCustomToolCall, etc.)
- Fix cascading build errors from regeneration (status type renames, constructor
  changes, read-only Id properties, CreatedBy BinaryData mismatch)
- Hand-maintain AgentServerResponsesModelFactory in Custom/ (emitter version
  had constructor parameter ordering mismatches)
- Update validation overlay to make 'id' optional for input item types (GAP-06)
- Simplify ApiErrorFactory.ParseResponseErrorCode — extensible struct accepts
  any string, no dictionary mapping needed
- Use storage_error code for persistence failure paths
- Update Generate-Contracts.ps1 to remove emitter-generated factory after copy
- All 2187 tests pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: Update model factory name in AGENTS.md and handler guide

Rename AzureAIAgentServerResponsesModelFactory → AgentServerResponsesModelFactory
to match the regenerated type name.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: Make AgentServerResponsesModelFactory internal, add ResponseUsage method

The factory is only used internally (ApiErrorFactory, ResponseEndpointHandler,
ResponsesModelFactory). No external consumer need — make it internal to keep
the public API surface minimal per Principle III.

- Add ResponseUsage factory method (with default detail objects)
- Update handler-implementation-guide to use ResponseUsage constructor directly
- Update AGENTS.md reference

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Address PR review feedback — cspell, constants, named args, changelog

- Add MCPHTTP to cspell.yaml (fixes CI spelling failure)
- Use PlatformHeaders constants in RequestIdMiddlewareTests
- Name trailing positional arg in ResponseCreatedEvent factory call
- Fix cref for now-internal AgentServerResponsesModelFactory
- Clarify CHANGELOG: transport exception still propagates

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: DELETE persistence-failed responses should clean up storage

In background mode, Phase 1 (CreateResponse) may succeed before
Phase 2 (UpdateResponse) fails. The response exists in storage
and must be cleaned up on DELETE — not just evicted from tracker.

- Attempt best-effort provider.DeleteResponseAsync (ignore NotFound)
- Attempt best-effort stream provider.DeleteEventStreamAsync
- Return standard 200 + DeleteResponseResult JSON (not 204)
- Add test: Background_UpdateFailure_DeleteCleansUpStorageAndTracker

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"Ravi Pidaparthi","email":"rapida@microsoft.com","date":"2026-04-22T19:04:19Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-22T19:04:19Z"}},"author":{"login":"RaviPidaparthi","id":28370364,"profile_url":"https://github.com/RaviPidaparthi","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/28370364?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}}]

arguments.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` **Triggered by:** PR #58396 by @RaviPidaparthi ## What Changed PR #58396 added the following to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (released as `1.0.0-beta.23`): - **New public type `PlatformHeaders`** — a static class centralizing all platform HTTP header name constants (`x-request-id`, `x-platform-server`, `x-agent-session-id`, isolation keys, `traceparent`, `x-ms-client-request-id`) - **`RequestIdMiddleware`** — sets the `x-request-id` response header automatically on every HTTP response (OTEL trace ID → incoming header → GUID) - **`RequestIdBaggagePropagator`** — propagates `x-request-id` into `Activity.Baggage` for distributed tracing correlation - **Simplified Tier 3 API** — replaced 6 individual `Add/Use` extension methods with a single `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair - **Breaking change**: Removed `IsolationContext.UserIsolationKeyHeaderName` and `IsolationContext.ChatIsolationKeyHeaderName`; use `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` and `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` instead ## Gaps Found - `PlatformHeaders` is a **new public API** but is absent from the README Key Concepts section — Tier 3 users and protocol-package authors who reference header name constants will not discover it from the README - The `x-request-id` automatic correlation behavior (RequestIdMiddleware sets it on every response using OTEL trace ID → incoming header → GUID fallback) is undocumented beyond a one-line changelog entry; users debugging distributed traces need this explained - The README Key Concepts section still lists `AddAgentServerVersion()` / `AddAgentServerLogging()` in the Logging paragraph but does not explain the new unified `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair, which replaces six calls for Tier 3 setups - No snippet or example shows how to read or use `PlatformHeaders` constants in custom middleware or Tier 3 setups - The `samples/Sample2_Configuration.md` sample file does not mention `PlatformHeaders` or the `x-request-id` correlation pattern
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`** — Edit Add a new **`PlatformHeaders`** entry to the Key Concepts section (after the `Health endpoint` subsection and before the `## Examples` section): ```markdown ### PlatformHeaders `PlatformHeaders` is a static class that centralises all HTTP header name constants used across the AgentServer platform. Protocol packages and custom middleware should reference these constants rather than hard-coding header name strings. | Constant | Header | Direction | |---|---|---| | `PlatformHeaders.RequestId` | `x-request-id` | Request (client correlation) / Response (always set) | | `PlatformHeaders.ServerVersion` | `x-platform-server` | Response (server identity) | | `PlatformHeaders.SessionId` | `x-agent-session-id` | Response (resolved session) | | `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` | `x-agent-user-isolation-key` | Request (platform-injected) | | `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` | `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` | Request (platform-injected) | | `PlatformHeaders.TraceParent` | `traceparent` | Request (W3C Trace Context) | | `PlatformHeaders.ClientRequestId` | `x-ms-client-request-id` | Request (Azure SDK correlation) | The `x-request-id` header is automatically set on every response by `RequestIdMiddleware` (included in both `AgentHostBuilder` and the `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` Tier 3 setup). The value is resolved as: OTEL trace ID → incoming `x-request-id` header → new GUID. Downstream code can read the resolved value from `HttpContext.Items[PlatformHeaders.RequestIdItemKey]`. ``` Also, update the existing **Logging** paragraph in Key Concepts to clarify `AddAgentServerCore()`: Find and replace this sentence: ``` for Tier 3, call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()` to enable it. ``` with: ``` for Tier 3 (standalone ASP.NET Core hosts), call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()`. These two calls replace the six individual extension methods that existed in earlier beta versions and register request-ID correlation, version headers, and inbound request logging in one step. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Add a new snippet demonstrating `PlatformHeaders` usage in a Tier 3 (standalone) setup. **Test file:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Samples/Sample3_PlatformHeaders.cs` Create this file: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core; using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder; using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Tests.Samples; public class Sample3_PlatformHeaders { [Test] [Ignore("Illustrative sample — not a runnable test")] public void PlatformHeaders_Tier3Setup() { #region Snippet:Core_ReadMe_PlatformHeaders var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(); // AddAgentServerCore registers RequestIdMiddleware, server-version header, and inbound logging. builder.Services.AddAgentServerCore(); var app = builder.Build(); app.UseAgentServerCore(); // Read the resolved request-correlation ID from HttpContext.Items in your own middleware. app.Use(async (context, next) => { if (context.Items.TryGetValue(PlatformHeaders.RequestIdItemKey, out var requestId)) { // requestId matches the x-request-id response header for this request. context.Response.Headers[PlatformHeaders.RequestId] = requestId?.ToString(); } await next(context); }); #endregion } } ``` Add a snippet placeholder in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` inside the `## Examples` section: ```markdown ### Using PlatformHeaders constants in a Tier 3 setup ```C# Snippet:Core_ReadMe_PlatformHeaders ``` ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` currently contains all required sections. The changes above are additions within existing sections, so no sections need to be created or reordered. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — injects snippet content from the new test file into the README placeholder 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no new public types were added, but run to confirm the API listing is current 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
arguments.title: [AgentServer] Docs: Document PlatformHeaders and x-request-id correlation in Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core README
